title: The 47-Day Streak I'm Proud I Lost

content.setup: I had a 47-day meditation streak. I was SO proud of it. Then I got the flu and missed three days. When I recovered, I couldn't bring myself to start again. That perfect number was gone.
content.development: For two weeks, I did nothing. The shame of 'starting over at zero' paralyzed me. I'd gone from 47 days to nothing because I couldn't handle imperfection.
content.turning_point: My therapist said something I'll never forget: 'You're not starting over. You're starting again. Your brain still has 47 days of practice. You're starting at 47, not zero.'
content.lesson: Streaks are tools, not goals. The moment they become the goal, they can break you. Missing once is data. Missing twice starts a pattern. But you're never at zero.
content.application: If you've broken a streak and feel like a failure - hear me: you're not starting over. Every day you practiced still counts. The only failure is not starting again.
